name: Cova des Migdia
starting_point: From the Ses Barques viewpoint, follow the signposted path towards Sa Costera, leaving it at Balitx d'Amunt. Continue parallel to the property wall to the Tanca des Bous crossroads, then straight ahead until the path ends at a well. Take the wide track to the right for 40-50 m to a dry stone wall on the left, follow it to its end, then continue parallel to the cliff veering right to a pine-surrounded clearing.
equipment: None essential, though for safety a helmet is recommended
description: A large single-chamber cave on a pine-surrounded clearing near Sóller, similar to Avenc de Son Pou. Features a massive entrance with stone stairs for comfortable access, weathered formations, and fig trees growing inside from pigeon droppings. The cave is classified as a site of special historical-archaeological interest and can be combined with an ascent of Puig de Balitx.
full_description:
The Cova des Migdia or des Coloms is located on a flat area where it is easy to walk, surrounded by pines, precisely in a place where rocky terrain with large cliffs and very rugged ground abounds.

If you already know the Avenc de Son Pou, this cave is very similar to it, as it consists of a single large chamber with a large entrance oriented to the southwest, if I remember correctly at the time of writing these lines. Accessing the interior of the cave can be done comfortably, as stone stairs have been built for this purpose.

A large fig tree has grown inside the cavity next to the stairs, and another small one grows on the walls, possibly due to the droppings of pigeons that roost or have previously roosted there.

More or less in the center of the cavity, some interesting formations remain standing in this cave, although very eroded and covered with moss, as well as some flowstones here and there.

The cave is of very large dimensions and makes us feel very small and defenseless when we walk through its interior trying to locate some crawlway that would allow us to access another area, but the search is in vain since the entire cave is within sight.

A visit is highly recommended and can be complemented with the ascent of Puig de Balitx (this excursion is described on the Mallorca Verde website), and if we don't want to follow the website's description, we can do it by accessing the summit from the cavity, which can be done in a short time although with a steep climb.

The Cova des Migdia is classified as a natural site of special historical-archaeological interest.
